duration: Several times over a few minutes with one minute of "viewing time,"
description: Swainson's Thrushes were everywhere at Crow Valley that morning, and among them, several Veery. David Dowell and I flushed up a bird that we thought was a Veery as it flew away based on it rusty upperpart color (in distinct contrast with two Olive-backed Thrushes that flushed up with it). We saw the bird land, and to our surprise, it had a buffy face with a weak buffy eyering. The spotting on the chest was more restricted and diffuse than that of Olive-backed Thrush, with a healthy wash of buff across chest. The sides had a buff wash to them.Behavior: As with other thrushes presentCall: not heardPlumage: unknown
